title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: A science fiction epic set on the desert planet Arrakis with politics, ecology, and survival.

title: Foundation
author: Isaac Asimov
description: A mathematician predicts the fall of a galactic empire and creates a foundation to preserve knowledge.

title: The Three-Body Problem
author: Liu Cixin
description: First contact science fiction with physics, strategy, and civilization conflict.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A brief history of humankind from cognitive revolution to modern society.

title: Guns, Germs, and Steel
author: Jared Diamond
description: Explains why civilizations developed differently through geography, biology, and technology.

title: The Silk Roads
author: Peter Frankopan
description: A global history centered on trade routes connecting East and West.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: A practical framework for building good habits and breaking bad ones.

title: The Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
description: How entrepreneurs use continuous innovation and validated learning to build startups.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
description: Rules for focused success in a distracted world.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A fantasy adventure about Bilbo Baggins and a quest to reclaim treasure from a dragon.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: A gifted young man's journey through magic, music, and myth.

title: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
description: An astronaut stranded on Mars uses engineering and science to survive.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
description: A cyberpunk novel about hackers, artificial intelligence, and corporate power.

title: Snow Crash
author: Neal Stephenson
description: A fast-paced cyberpunk story blending virtual worlds, language, and memetic contagion.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian classic about surveillance, propaganda, and authoritarian control.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A dystopian vision of a technologically managed society and engineered happiness.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: An exploration of two cognitive systems shaping judgment and decision-making.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
description: Stories about behavior, risk, and long-term thinking in personal finance.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: Principles and practices for writing readable, maintainable software.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: A deep guide to data systems, distributed architecture, and reliability.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: An accessible introduction to cosmology, black holes, and the nature of time.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A sweeping narrative of science, astronomy, and humanity's place in the universe.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt
description: Practical advice for software craftsmanship, habits, and career growth.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
description: Perspectives on building startups and creating unique value in markets.
